figures.caption: Fig. 1. Motivation: Texture-sensitive representations cause high-frequency boundary errors. (a) Baseline features shift under appearance changes. (b) Our features remain domain-invariant. (c) Baseline shows boundary misalignment. (d) Errors concentrate in high-frequency bands. (e) Our method preserves fine boundaries.
Fig. 6. Qualitative comparison of segmentation results on Kvasir-SEG, ISIC-2016, and GlaS.
Fig. 5. Robustness analysis against appearance corruptions on Kvasir-SEG.

Fig. 2. Overview of the proposed two-stage framework. (a) Stage-1 performs geometryguided latent representation pretraining with MeanFlow and Dispersive Loss; (b) Stage2 adopts a lightweight decoder with DAF-based fusion and FDConv refinement; (c) illustrates the structure of the DAF module.
Fig. 8. Sensitivity Analysis of the Dispersive Loss Weight
